mapping.a_element: stationary state maintained far from equilibrium by continuous drive plus dissipation
mapping.b_element: self-maintaining far-from-equilibrium organization sustained by free-energy throughput

mapping.a_element: local dissipation supplied by weakly dispersive bosonic modes
mapping.b_element: regulated noise sensitivity / sustained free-energy throughput

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the generic theme of a non-equilibrium steady state sustained by external driving and dissipation; the actual machinery differs. Paper A is an open-quantum-system dissipative-engineering protocol (layer-pseudospin pairing prepared as a Lindblad steady state, with a superradiant burst under collective dissipation), whereas paper B is a conceptual diagnostic phase-space of necessary conditions (organizational closure, active information maintenance) for classifying physical autonomy/life, so no coherent role-for-role dynamical mapping holds — A has no notion of closure or self-maintenance and B has no pairing or quantum dissipative dynamics.
